content:
# Numbers Chapter 15 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 166-167 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_015_page_001.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0123-0124

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land where you will settle, which I am giving to you,

**3** and you prepare a fire offering to YHWH -- a burnt offering or a sacrifice, whether fulfilling a special vow, or as a freewill offering, or at your appointed festivals -- to produce a soothing aroma for YHWH, from the herd or from the flock,

**4** then the one presenting his offering to YHWH shall bring as a grain offering one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-quarter of a hin of oil.

**5** And wine for the drink offering -- one-quarter of a hin -- you shall prepare with the burnt offering or for the sacrifice, for each lamb.

**6** Or for a ram, you shall prepare as a grain offering two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-third of a hin of oil,

**7** and for the drink offering you shall present one-third of a hin of wine, as a soothing aroma to YHWH.

**8** And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or as a sacrifice, whether fulfilling a special vow or as a peace offering to YHWH,

**9** then he shall bring with the young bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with half a hin of oil.

**10** And for the drink offering you shall present half a hin of wine -- a fire offering of soothing aroma to YHWH.

**11** This is what shall be done for each bull, or for each ram, or for each young sheep or goat.

**12** According to the number that you prepare, so shall you do for each one, according to their number.

**13** Every native-born shall do these things in this way when presenting a fire offering of soothing aroma to YHWH.

**14** And when a foreigner resides among you, or whoever is in your midst throughout your generations, and he prepares a fire offering of soothing aroma to YHWH -- just as you do, so shall he do.

**15** As for the assembly, there shall be one statute for you and for the foreigner who resides among you -- an eternal statute throughout your generations. As you are, so shall the foreigner be before YHWH.

**16** One law and one standard of justice shall apply to you and to the foreigner who resides among you."

**17** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**18** "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I am bringing you,

**19** and when you eat from the bread of the land, you shall set aside a contribution for YHWH.

**20** From the first of your dough you shall set aside a loaf as a contribution -- just as you set aside the contribution from the threshing floor, so shall you set this aside.

**21** From the first of your dough you shall give a contribution to YHWH throughout your generations.

**22** And when you go astray and fail to observe all these commandments that YHWH has spoken to Moses --

**23** everything that YHWH has commanded you through Moses, from the day YHWH first gave commandment and onward throughout your generations --

**24** if it was done unintentionally, out of the awareness of the community, then the whole community shall offer one young bull as a burnt offering, as a soothing aroma to YHWH, along with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the established rule, and one male goat as a sin offering.

**25** And the priest shall make atonement for the whole community of the Sons of Israel, and they shall be forgiven, for it was unintentional. And they shall bring their offering -- a fire offering to YHWH -- along with their sin offering before YHWH for their unintentional error.

**26** And the whole community of the Sons of Israel shall be forgiven, as well as the foreigner who resides among them, for the entire people acted unintentionally.

**27** And if a single person sins unintentionally, that person shall present a female goat in its first year as a sin offering.

**28** And the priest shall make atonement before YHWH for the person who went astray by sinning unintentionally, making atonement on their behalf, and they shall be forgiven.

**29** For the native-born among the Sons of Israel and for the foreigner who resides among them -- one law shall apply to you all regarding the one who acts unintentionally.

**30** But the person who acts defiantly, whether native-born or foreigner -- that one blasphemes YHWH, and that person shall be cut off from among their people.

**31** Because they have despised the word of YHWH and broken his commandment, that person shall be utterly cut off -- their guilt remains upon them."

**32** And while the Sons of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering wood on the Sabbath day.

**33** And those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses and to Aaron and to the whole community.

**34** And they placed him under guard, because it had not yet been declared what should be done to him.

**35** And YHWH said to Moses, "The man must surely be put to death. The whole community shall stone him with stones outside the camp."

**36** And the whole community brought him outside the camp and stoned him with stones, and he died -- just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**37** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**38** "Speak to the Sons of Israel and tell them to make for themselves tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and they shall put a cord of blue-violet on the tassel of each corner.

**39** And it shall serve as a tassel for you, and when you look at it, you will remember all the commandments of YHWH and do them, so that you do not go exploring after your own heart and after your own eyes, which you are prone to chase after unfaithfully.

**40** So that you may remember and do all my commandments, and be holy to your God.

**41** I am YHWH your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am YHWH your God."

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 3 -- "soothing aroma" (ריח ניחח):** The Hebrew *re'ach nichoach* literally means "an aroma of rest/pleasantness." I have rendered this as "soothing aroma" throughout the chapter to capture the idea that the offering brings satisfaction or acceptance to YHWH, rather than merely describing a physical smell. The LXX (Vaticanus) uses *osme euodias* ("fragrance of sweet smell"), confirming the sense of pleasing acceptance.

**Verse 20 -- "first of your dough" (ראשית עריסתיכם):** The Hebrew *arisah* is a rare word appearing only in this passage and Ezekiel 44:30. It refers to the first batch of dough or coarse meal. I have rendered it "first of your dough" to convey the idea of the initial portion being sacred, which captures the dynamic force of the *reshit* (firstfruits) principle.

**Verse 24 -- "out of the awareness of the community":** The Hebrew *me'eynei ha'edah* literally reads "from the eyes of the community," meaning the sin was committed without the community's knowledge or perception. I rendered this dynamically to convey that the error escaped communal notice, rather than using the more wooden "from the eyes of."

**Verse 30 -- "acts defiantly" (ביד רמה):** Literally "with a high/raised hand," this idiom describes brazen, deliberate rebellion -- acting with open defiance rather than stumbling through ignorance. I have used "acts defiantly" to capture the force of the Hebrew idiom, which contrasts sharply with the *bishgagah* ("unintentionally") of the preceding section. The LXX reads *en cheiri huperephanias* ("with a hand of arrogance"), reinforcing the sense of willful pride.

**Verse 31 -- "utterly cut off" (הכרת תכרת):** This is the infinitive absolute construction (*hikaret tikkaret*), which I preserve with the doubling "utterly cut off" per our conventions. The intensification signals the severity and finality of the consequence for deliberate covenant violation.

**Verse 38 -- "blue-violet" (תכלת):** The Hebrew *tekhelet* refers to a specific blue-purple dye extracted from a marine snail (*Murex trunculus*). I render it "blue-violet" rather than simply "blue" to better approximate the actual hue attested in archaeological dye analysis, and to distinguish it from ordinary blue.

**Verse 39 -- "go exploring after your own heart and after your own eyes, which you are prone to chase after unfaithfully":** The Hebrew *lo taturu acharei levavkhem v'acharei eineikhem asher atem zonim achareihem* uses the verb *tur* ("to scout/explore") -- the same verb used for the spies in chapters 13-14 -- creating a powerful literary echo. The scouts explored the land and led Israel astray; now Israel is warned not to "scout" after their own desires. The verb *zonim* ("going astray after") carries connotations of sexual infidelity and idolatrous wandering. I rendered this dynamically to preserve both the sense of pursuit and the unfaithfulness implied by *zanah*.

**Verses 15-16 -- Equal law for native and foreigner:** The emphatic repetition of "one law" (*torah achat*) and "one standard of justice" (*mishpat echad*) for both native-born and resident foreigner is a remarkable statement of legal equality. I have preserved the parallelism to let its force speak.

**Context note:** This chapter follows immediately after the devastating rebellion of chapters 13-14, where the generation was condemned to die in the wilderness. The opening phrase "When you enter the land" (v. 2) is a deliberate message of hope -- YHWH still intends to bring Israel into the land, despite the judgment. The chapter's placement is theologically significant: laws for life in the land are given precisely when entrance to the land has been delayed by a generation.

-- PNEUMA
